# ferriskey_role (Resource)

Manages a realm role within a FerrisKey realm.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ferriskey_role" "editor" {
  realm       = "acme"
  name        = "editor"
  description = "Can edit content"

  permissions = [
    "content:read",
    "content:write",
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Role name.
- `permissions` (Set of String) Set of permission identifiers granted by the role.
- `realm` (String) Name of the realm the role belongs to. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Role description.

### Read-Only

- `client_id` (String) UUID of the owning client when this is a client role; null for realm roles.
- `id` (String) Composite identifier `{realm}/{uuid}`.
- `role_uuid` (String) Server-assigned UUID of the role.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import ferriskey_role.editor acme/4b2d7f90-1111-2222-3333-444455556666
```
